title: Tangy Crunchy Peruvian Beef Crumble
description: A tangy and crunchy brunch dish featuring mixed beef cuts with a fermentation twist, nut-free-friendly.

ingredients.name: Fermented Vegetables (e.g., Kimchi)
ingredients.notes: Use sparingly as per taste preference

ingredients.name: Vegetable Oil for Pan-Frying
ingredients.notes: Adjust quantity based on pan size

instructions: Pat the beef brisket dry with paper towels.
Season both sides of the beef with salt and pepper, then coat in a thin layer of vegetable oil to prevent sticking.
Heat a large skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add enough vegetable oil to lightly coat the bottom of the pan.
Once the oil is hot, place the seasoned brisket into the pan. Cook for about 5 minutes on each side until charred and cooked through. Remove from heat and set aside to rest.
In a mixing bowl, combine tahini, lemon juice, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt. Stir well until there are no lumps left.
Slice the rested brisket into bite-sized pieces and fold in with the tahini mixture. Add any desired fermented vegetables for additional texture.
Serve immediately as a brunch dish or cold as a snack.
